creator: fernape@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Graham Perrin ◐ from comment #20)
So, before:

> 1.4.30_1 crashes after updating the system, 1.4.30 does not crash. 

Now even 1.4.30 crashes?

Did you try with freeRDP directly?(In reply to Graham Perrin ◐ from comment #20)

> [10:23:37:429] [8186:79615100] [ERROR][com.freerdp.common.settings] -
> [freerdp_settings_get_bool] Invalid key index 131
> [10:23:37:429] [8186:79615100] [ERROR][com.freerdp.common.settings] -
> [freerdp_settings_get_bool] Invalid key index 0


> Google finds a few matches, for example <https://gitlab.com/Remmina/Remmina
>/-/issues/2580> however the steps there differ, and so on.

I don't think that has anything to do with the crash. Those lines from freerdp don't make remmina crash. The issue you are pointing out was a crash due to a bad destroy of a gtk object when closing the connection.

> Program terminated with signal SIGBUS, Bus error.
> Object-specific hardware error.
> #0  check_and_init_rwlock (rwlock=0x483f8b48e5894855, rwlock_out=0x8af01c8f8) > at /usr/src/lib/libthr/thread/thr_rwlock.c:68
> 68              if (__predict_false(*rwlock == THR_PSHARED_PTR ||
> [Current thread is 1 (LWP 104241)]
> (gdb) bt

That is really awful. Something is truly wrong (a misaligned data or something). This goes down to thr_create and passes through openssl. I don't think this has anything to do with remmina itself.

For this I would:

1. - Try to connect with vanilla freerdp2
2. - Try to connect via RDP to an unsecure host without the need of using a certificate (to rule out if this is a problem in openssl)
3. - Try to connect in a vanilla FreeBSD. This means without changes to the scheduler to the very least.
